objective: Turn Codex plugin interest into proof-backed installs, Pro checkout starts, and qualified workflow sprint conversations.

canonicalIdentity.shortDescription: Auto-updating ThumbGate plugin for Codex. Capture thumbs-up/down feedback, turn repeated failures into Pre-Action Checks, and keep proof close to the install path.

listingCopy.headline: Stop Codex from repeating the same tool mistake.
listingCopy.subhead: ThumbGate gives Codex an auto-updating MCP runtime, local-first feedback memory, and hard pre-action gates before risky commands, edits, or publishes run again.
listingCopy.shortDescription: Auto-updating ThumbGate plugin for Codex. Capture thumbs-up/down feedback, turn repeated failures into Pre-Action Checks, and keep proof close to the install path.
proofBullets: Auto-updating Codex MCP runtime resolves thumbgate@latest on startup.
Typed thumbs-up/down feedback can become durable Pre-Action Checks instead of chat-only reminders.
Commercial Truth and Verification Evidence stay one click from the install path.

followOnOffers.label: ThumbGate Pro
followOnOffers.buyer: Solo Codex operators who proved one blocked repeat and want the personal dashboard plus proof-ready exports.

followOnOffers.label: Workflow Hardening Sprint
followOnOffers.pricing: Intake-led sprint, then Team at $49/seat/mo with 3-seat minimum after qualification
followOnOffers.buyer: Teams that already named one repeated workflow failure, one owner, and one approval boundary.

operatorQueue.persona: Solo Codex operator with one repeated tool or file-edit mistake
operatorQueue.evidence: README.md promotes npx thumbgate init --agent codex and public/codex-plugin.html keeps the direct plugin path visible.
operatorQueue.proofTrigger: They can name one repeat they would pay to block before the next tool call.
operatorQueue.recommendedMotion: Install the plugin or run the CLI, prove one blocked repeat, then offer Pro.

operatorQueue.persona: Team evaluating repo-backed Codex rollout instead of ad hoc local config edits

operatorQueue.recommendedMotion: Qualify the team for the Workflow Hardening Sprint before pitching Team expansion.

outreachDrafts.draft: You already have Codex. The missing piece is blocking one repeated mistake before the next tool call, not adding another memory note. ThumbGate now has a direct Codex install page, standalone bundle, and proof links in one place: https://thumbgate-production.up.railway.app/codex-plugin?utm_source=codex&utm_medium=plugin_page&utm_campaign=codex_outreach_install&utm_content=plugin_page&campaign_variant=install_follow_up&offer_code=CODEX-OUTREACH_INSTALL&cta_id=codex_outreach_install&cta_placement=outreach_draft&surface=codex_plugin . If you can name one repeated command or file-edit failure, that page gives the fastest path to prove the block locally.

I am not pitching another agent platform. I am pitching one Codex-adjacent workflow that becomes safe enough to ship because the repeated mistake gets turned into a Pre-Action Check and the proof stays inspectable. If your team already has one owner and one repeated approval-boundary failure, the next useful step is the Workflow Hardening Sprint intake: https://thumbgate-production.up.railway.app/?utm_source=codex&utm_medium=plugin_page&utm_campaign=codex_outreach_sprint&utm_content=workflow_sprint&campaign_variant=proof_first_team&offer_code=CODEX-OUTREACH_SPRINT&cta_id=codex_outreach_sprint&cta_placement=outreach_draft&surface=codex_outreach#workflow-sprint-intake .

outreachDrafts.audience: Claude Code team evaluating Codex as a second reviewer
outreachDrafts.draft: If the team already trusts Claude Code but wants a Codex second-pass lane, ThumbGate keeps the same local feedback memory while Codex handles review or adversarial review. The bridge surface is here: https://github.com/IgorGanapolsky/ThumbGate/blob/main/plugins/claude-codex-bridge/README.md . If that maps to a real workflow with one owner, route it to the sprint intake instead of a generic plugin pitch.

measurementPlan.policy: Treat Codex installs as useful only when they produce a tracked Pro checkout start or a qualified sprint conversation.
guardrails: Do not claim installs, revenue, or marketplace approval without direct command evidence.
Do not pitch Team before the buyer names one repeated workflow failure and one owner.
Keep pricing and traction claims aligned with COMMERCIAL_TRUTH.md.
